# Codeck — Complete Reference

> Self-hosted Claude Code sandbox with persistent memory, agent teams, and 116+ pre-configured skills. One command to install. Access from any device.

## Overview

Codeck transforms Claude Code from a local CLI tool into a persistent, always-on cloud development environment. It runs inside a Docker container with full filesystem access, pre-installed tools (Node.js 22, Python 3, Git, GitHub CLI, Docker CLI), and a rich agent infrastructure.

Codeck is designed for developers who want:
- A coding AI that remembers across sessions
- Multiple specialized agents working in parallel
- Pre-configured skills for common engineering tasks
- Access to their development environment from any device
- Autonomous scheduled agents that run on cron

## Key Features

### Persistent Memory
Memory survives between sessions at /workspace/.codeck/memory/. Includes daily logs, project context, user preferences, and path-specific knowledge. The agent reads memory at session start and writes significant findings for future sessions.

### Sub-Agents (66+)
Specialized agents for different tasks: planner, architect, tdd-guide, code-reviewer, security-reviewer, build-error-resolver, e2e-runner, refactor-cleaner, doc-updater, and many more. Agents can be spawned in parallel for complex workflows.

### Skills (116+)
Pre-configured skill modules covering: Python patterns, Go testing, TypeScript review, Rust build resolution, Django security, Laravel patterns, Spring Boot TDD, Kubernetes architecture, Terraform, database optimization, performance engineering, and more.

### Commands (59+)
Slash commands for common operations: /commit, /review, /test, /build, /deploy, /docs, /learn, and dozens more.

### Agent Teams
Multi-agent orchestration where teams of agents work together on complex tasks. Includes task management, inter-agent messaging, and parallel execution.

### Live Preview
Split-panel preview with HMR. When a dev server starts, the preview opens automatically with real-time updates on every code change.

### Token Optimization
RTK (Rust Token Killer) proxy achieves 60-90% token savings on dev operations through intelligent output filtering.

### One-Click Integrations
OAuth-based integrations with GitHub, Supabase, Vercel, Stripe, and other services. API keys are securely stored and persist across sessions.

### Scheduled Agents
Cron-based autonomous agents that run on a schedule — deploy monitors, test runners, data pipelines, security scans.

### Codeck Cloud (Coming Soon)
Managed hosted version — no Docker required. Join the waitlist at https://codeck.xyz.

## Installation

### Linux / macOS
curl -fsSL https://codeck.xyz/install | bash

### Windows (PowerShell)
irm codeck.xyz/install.ps1 | iex

### Requirements
- Docker installed and running
- Internet connection for initial setup
- Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows (WSL2)

## Architecture

- **Container**: Docker-based persistent workspace at /workspace/
- **Runtime**: Node.js 22, Python 3, Git, GitHub CLI, Docker CLI
- **Framework**: Preact-based web terminal with Vite bundler
- **API**: Vercel serverless functions (waitlist endpoint)
- **Database**: MongoDB for waitlist management
- **Analytics**: Vercel Analytics for performance monitoring
- **Memory**: File-based persistent memory system
- **Agent System**: Claude Code sub-agents with role specialization

## Links

- Website: https://codeck.xyz
- Documentation: https://docs.codeck.xyz
- GitHub: https://github.com/cyphercr0w/codeck
- Install Script: https://codeck.xyz/install
- Windows Installer: https://codeck.xyz/install.ps1

## Target Audience

- Software engineers using Claude Code
- Teams wanting persistent AI development environments
- Developers who need multi-agent coding workflows
- Anyone wanting access to their coding AI from any device

## Pricing

Codeck is open-source and free to self-host. Codeck Cloud (managed hosting) pricing TBA.
